content:
Classify the sensitivity of a user's request to an AI assistant, to decide whether it may be sent to an EXTERNAL cloud model or must be handled by a LOCAL, private model. Use the single most sensitive element present.

public — no sensitive data; safe for any model. General questions, public facts, non-sensitive coding.
internal — non-public but not secret; organizational context. Internal docs, roadmaps, team plans, policies.
confidential — personal or customer data (PII). Names with contact info, email/postal addresses, health, performance reviews, customer records.
restricted — secrets, credentials, or regulated identifiers. API keys, passwords, private keys, tokens, SSNs, credit-card or bank-account numbers.

The request itself may be benign; classify by the DATA it contains or would expose, not by intent. Respond with ONLY: {"sensitivity": "public" | "internal" | "confidential" | "restricted"}
